findings.title: .wrangler/ directory not gitignored
findings.why_it_matters: Wrangler creates a .wrangler/ directory containing local state (cache, credentials, deployment IDs) when deployed or run with `wrangler dev`. Without a gitignore entry, this directory will appear as untracked files and could accidentally be committed. Other Cloudflare projects universally gitignore .wrangler/.
evidence: wrangler.toml was just introduced — .wrangler/ directory will be created on first wrangler deploy or wrangler dev

residual_risks: The shared retentionPolicy.ts must be manually kept in sync with the browser module. The existing parity test only checks the browser module's self-consistency, not the Deno copy.
